assertions.a: Walks seven phases in order
Phase 1 cheap-check triage (Waiting on Author before deep review)
Doc update under doc/src/sgml/config.sgml
Flags ABI implications of inline functions/macros in src/include/
Revert-just-the-code sanity test for the new test
Concrete-asks discipline
CF state transition
Cross-references sibling skills (locking, error-handling, memory-contexts, coding-style, catalog-conventions)
XLOG_PAGE_MAGIC bump
rmgrlist.h registration with full callback set
crash-recovery TAP test under src/test/recovery/
Lock order in redo matches write path
Master-only / not backpatchable
No palloc in critical section + XLogReadBufferForRedo
Hands off to wal-and-xlog deep skill
Hint-bit MarkBufferDirtyHint vs MarkBufferDirty correctness
Cross-references locking + catalog-conventions + testing skills
Release-mode build for benchmarking
Multiple runs + median + same hardware/flags
Regression hunt on unrelated workloads
Microbenchmark vs end-to-end honesty
Self-review specifics (commit split, no debug elog, no commented-out code)
Assertion-build test pass separately from perf run
Post numbers as a table + exact recipe
O(N^2) / scaling at larger N footgun reminder
Hands off to patch-submission + commit-message-style for final mailing

notes: Iter-2 adds 3 new assertions specifically targeting the gap proposed-edits.md flagged (sibling-skill cross-references). All three pass.

Skill now does what an orchestration skill should: triage + handoff. Specialist depth lives in the named sibling skills.
